title: QA: Quality-gate runner shells timeout+command via bash -c without separation (guardrails.rs)
description:
## Severity
Critical
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/guardrails.rs:492-541
## Violation
`run_shell_command_sync` builds and executes a shell string by
interpolating two attacker-influenced fields:
```rust
let actual_command = if timeout_seconds > 0 {
    format!("timeout {timeout_seconds} {command}")
} else {
    command.to_string()
};
...
Command::new("bash").args(["-c", &actual_command])
```
`command` is `QualityCheck.command` (config-loaded). Any check that
templates user-supplied tooling runs through a layered shell with zero
escaping or allowlisting. There is no audit log beyond the bare
`info!(name = %check.name, "Running quality gate")`; stderr is captured
but not surfaced.
## Mandated refactor
Drop `bash -c`. Require `QualityCheck { program: String, args: Vec<String> }`
and exec via `Command::new(program).args(args)`. If timeout wrapping is
required, build it as `Command::new("timeout").args([ts.to_string(),
program]).args(args)`. Emit a structured audit-log event for every
quality-gate invocation with full argv before spawning.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
